Question 1 a: Number of affiliations with trade and industry chambers/ associations: 63
Question 1b: List the top 10 trade and industry chambers/ associations (determined based on the total members of such body) the entity is a member of/ affiliated to.
Question 2: There was no issue related to anti-competitive conduct by the entity during the year.
LEADERSHIP INDICATORS:
Question 1: L&T actively engages in public policy advocacy across multiple sectors, aligned with its business priorities and commitment to national development. The Company is a key participant in various committees of industry bodies such as FICCI and CII, as well as sector-specific councils covering areas such as energy storage, semiconductors, quality, environment and climate change, capital goods, transport, aerospace and roads and highways. In addition, the Company engages with nodal agencies, government ministries and other public institutions to provide sector-specific insights and technical expertise. Key areas of advocacy during the year included, but no limited to: • SHANTI (Sustainable Harnessing and Advancement of Nuclear Technology) Act, 2025: The Company engaged with the industry bodies, Department of Atomic Energy, NITI Aayog and relevant committees, over an extended period to provide technical and commercial inputs towards enabling a structured framework for private sector participation in India’s civilian nuclear energy sector. A few highlighted inputs provided are as follows: • Enabling licensing framework for private sector participation across the lifecycle of nuclear power projects (build, own, operate and decommission), with continued government control over sensitive activities. • Tiered operator liability structure with defined caps and a government-backed risk-sharing mechanism to facilitate commercial viability • Calibrated ‘Right of Recourse’ provisions to provide legal certainty to equipment suppliers and plant operators. Outcome: The SHANTI Bill, 2025 was approved by Parliament in December 2025. The enacted provisions are broadly aligned with industry recommendations, including private sector licensing, defined liability structures and government-controlled activity exclusions. • Reforms in Public Procurement Models: The Company, through industry associations, engaged with government stakeholders to advocate improvements in procurement frameworks to enhance quality, transparency and long-term value creation. A few highlighted inputs provided are as follows: • Transition from Least Cost (L1) and e-Reverse Auction mechanisms towards Quality and Cost Based Selection (QCBS); since the former tends to drive selection towards the lowest cost at the expense of technical competence and project quality. • Integration of ESG parameters and life-cycle costing in bid evaluation criteria to ensure sustainable and longterm value outcomes from public investment. • Policy measures to address abnormally low bids and improve project execution outcomes. • Jal Jeevan Mission: As an implementation partner in the Jal Jeevan Mission, the Company provided field-level insights through industry associations to support policy refinement and execution efficiency. A few highlighted inputs provided are: Adequate budgetary allocation and timely fund disbursement mechanisms. • Strengthening last-mile delivery through improved contractor capacity and logistics planning. • Enhanced inter-agency coordination and robust post-completion O&M frameworks to strengthen project monitoring and quality assurance. Outcome: JJM 2.0 was approved by the Union Cabinet in March 2026, with enhanced outlay and a revised service delivery framework. • Quality Control Orders (QCO) – Steel Raw Material Imports for BTG Manufacturing: The Company engaged with the Ministry of Steel and industry bodies regarding Quality Control Orders impacting the import of specialised steel inputs required for Boilers, Turbines and Generators (BTG). A few highlighted inputs provided are as follows: • Representations to the government documenting the specific input materials affected, the absence of adequate domestic substitutes meeting requisite technical standards and the resulting impact on project execution timelines. • Advocating for a structured one-year transition period for materials in transit and under production, to prevent further supply chain disruptions. Outcome: The original QCO order was revoked. A clearance waiver was provided for consignments received up to July 2025. Engagement continues regarding transition provisions and future regulatory clarity. Ongoing areas: Policy advocacy remains a continuous and evolving process, with several priority areas from the previous year’s BRSR continuing to be actively pursued. Key topics such as the development of a green taxonomy framework, the role of Small Modular Reactors in advancing nuclear energy, the progression of the green hydrogen policy and mission and ongoing reforms in public procurement models remain crucial to the Company’s advocacy efforts.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheTradeAndIndustryChamb: Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce & Industry (FICCI)
Associated Chambers of Commerce and Industry of India (ASSOCHAM)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive:
The Company has established comprehensive grievance redressal mechanisms across its project sites, manufacturing facilities, campuses and offices to capture and address community concerns. Complaints are received through multiple channels, including designated personnel, administration and industrial relations teams and complaint/suggestion boxes placed at the locations. Community members may raise grievances either in writing or orally. Grievances are addressed based on their severity and complexity, with resolution timelines defined accordingly and outcomes communicated to the concerned stakeholders. Typically, grievances may be submitted in writing to the respective Project Head/Coordinator, who records, assesses and initiates corrective action.
The Company’s Whistleblower Mechanism also serves as an alternate channel, also available at https://www.larsentoubro.com/corporate-policies, ensuring multiple avenues for stakeholders to raise concerns. Additional channels include contact details available on the Company’s website and official social media platforms. The grievances are routed to relevant functions for resolution, with progress tracked until closure. Matters requiring escalation are referred to business heads for appropriate action.
CSR initiatives are implemented in partnership with NGOs, government agencies and internal teams across locations. A structured grievance mechanism is in place for CSR projects, enabling communities to raise concerns and ensuring timely and transparent resolution. Alternatively, grievances may also be submitted directly to Corporate CSR department through email at grievance.csr@larsentoubro.com, with a commitment to provide communication or resolution within 20 days. The Company actively seeks stakeholder feedback to strengthen CSR outcomes. Requests related to expansion of CSR scope, new project proposals or additional funding fall outside the scope of the grievance mechanism.

LEADERSHIP INDICATORS:-
Question 1:
No actions required by the Company.
Question 3 a:
The Company follows structured procurement practices aligned with its commitment to quality, sustainability and ethical standards. Its Code of Conduct for Suppliers and Sustainable Supply Chain Policy emphasise fair working conditions, environmental stewardship, human rights, responsible sourcing and ethical business practices across the supply chain. While the Company does not have a specific preferential procurement policy for supply chain partners from marginalised or vulnerable groups, existing processes support inclusive participation; for e.g. encouraging participation of micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) in bidding processes, promoting services from marginalised communities wherever feasible. However, given the nature of the Company’s EPC and high-tech manufacturing operations—which require large volumes of specialised industrial materials and services—procurement is largely undertaken from established large and mid-sized enterprises due to scale, quality and compliance requirements.
Question 3 c:
The Company engages with marginalised and vulnerable groups—such as women-led Self-Help Groups (SHGs), local farmers and small business owners—primarily for canteen-related supplies at its manufacturing facilities. However, procurement from these groups constitutes a relatively small share of the overall spend. This is attributable to the nature of the Company’s EPC and high-tech manufacturing operations, which require large volumes of industrial materials (e.g., cement, steel, fuel, pipes, cables, ready-mix concrete) and specialised services such as logistics, IT/ITES and manpower subcontracting. These requirements are typically met by large and mid-sized enterprises due to scale, quality and compliance considerations.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo:
L&T operates predominantly in the B2B (business-to-business) sector. As a leading conglomerate, the Company provides a wide range of services and solutions to various industries. There are multiple channels to collect consumer complaints and feedback.
• Communication: Customers can submit complaints or feedback through email, letters, customer complaint registers and even verbally directly to the business teams at various work locations of the Company. The Company also has a toll-free number and email address at infodesk@larsentoubro.com for collecting the customer inputs/feedback. Feedback from the customers is collected through a structured feedback form on a periodic basis. Format to record the complaints/feedback as well as SOPs to handle them are part of the Quality Management System implemented by various business units of the Company. Inputs received from the customers are categorized and forwarded to the relevant teams or departments. Each business unit maintains a record of complaints received and resolutions provided. These are reviewed at regular intervals at different management levels, starting from project teams and up to Business Head and Executive Committee level.
• Physical Offices: L&T has several offices across India where consumers can submit their concerns in person or via mail to the concerned department or person.
• L&T’s Investor Relations Cell: can be contacted at igrc@larsentoubro.com
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: No cases/complaints received in above matters
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfImpactOfDataBreachesE: There were no data breaches during the year.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WeblinkWhereInformationOnProdu:
L&T offers detailed information about its diverse products and services through various official channels: • Corporate Website: The primary channel for communicating the Company’s offerings is its official website at https://www.larsentoubro.com. ‘Products and Services’ section provides insights into the Company’s extensive range of solutions across sectors such as infrastructure, energy, oil & gas, process industries and other industries.
• Websites of the business verticals: Various businesses of the Company maintain dedicated websites that offer in-depth information on the business specific offerings: – L&T Construction: As one of the largest construction organizations globally, L&T Construction’s website details its capabilities for EPC projects in infrastructure and related sectors. – L&T Energy: showcases the capabilities of various businesses of L&T for execution of EPC projects in oil & gas sector, thermal and gas power, offshore wind and green hydrogen and derivatives. – L&T Heavy Engineering: showcases the Hi-Tech manufacturing capabilities related to equipment, subsystems or modules for process plants, nuclear power and other industries – Other businesses such as Rubber Processing Machinery, Construction & Mining Machinery, Valves, Shipbuilding, Precision Engineering & Systems, L&T-SuFin, L&T-EduTech, L&T Vyoma and more – L&T Realty: showcases the capabilities and offerings across different segments - residential, commercial and retail spaces - of real estate related business
• Annual Reports and Investor Presentations: L&T’s annual reports and investor presentations, accessible through the ‘Investors’ section of the Company website (https://investors.larsentoubro.com/), provide detailed overview of business performance, new contract wins, business strategy and initiatives.
• Social Media Platforms: L&T is present on platforms like LinkedIn, X (formerly Twitter) and YouTube, where the Company shares business updates, highlights from contracts under execution, CSR and sustainability initiatives, information on products and services and so on. Through these various channels, stakeholders can access detailed and up-to-date information on L&T’s diverse products and services across its various businesses.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StepsTakenToInformAndEducateCo: The Company does not operate in B2C (business-to-consumer) segment, with its offerings comprising engineered-toorder equipment, modules and sub-systems for process industries and related sectors. The Company engages regularly with customers to showcase its products, innovations, emerging technologies and advanced execution methodologies.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsInPlaceToInformConsu: The Company does not have a direct role in the provision of essential services. However, during execution of EPC project contracts and transportation of machinery/equipment related to such contracts, proactive coordination is undertaken with customers and relevant public authorities. Necessary approvals are obtained in advance for activities such as road closures, traffic diversions and temporary isolation of utilities, ensuring minimal disruption and safe execution.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TheEntityHasNotApplicableProdu:
The Company does not manufacture or sell products which are covered under such laws. L&T ensures that the relevant information is provided, which may include detailed technical specifications, safety guidelines, environmental considerations, quality certifications, and user instructions. For certain projects and solutions, especially in sectors like infrastructure, power, and heavy engineering, L&T also shares environmental performance data, sustainability impacts, and operation & maintenance insights with clients. These additional disclosures are aimed at enabling customers and end-users to make informed decisions, enhance safe usage, and support transparency and trust in the Company’s product and service delivery.
L&T regularly conducts customer satisfaction surveys across its key business verticals to gather insights on customer experience, service quality, and product performance, which are ingrained in the Quality Management Systems. These surveys are structured to assess customer feedback across major products and services and are conducted in significant locations of operation, including India and international markets. L&T’s customer feedback mechanisms include structured surveys, client review meetings, third-party assessments, and digital platforms, which collectively help in understanding client expectations and areas for improvement. The feedback is collected through a structured questionnaire based on relevant parameters and a 10-point Likert scale. Typically, feedback is collected on a half-yearly or annual basis.
Insights gathered from these surveys are reviewed by senior leadership and integrated into continuous improvement programmes across business units, ensuring alignment with our commitment to customer-centric excellence. Additionally, specific business verticals have dedicated teams that engage with clients and key stakeholders to measure satisfaction levels, address grievances, and enhance service delivery.The Company does not manufacture or sell products which are covered under such laws. Question 2:
The Company does not manufacture or sell consumer products. The products manufactured by the Company are engineered-to-order equipment, modules, sub-systems etc for process industries and other such sectors. All relevant information e.g., operating parameters, maintenance process are provided as part of the contractual requirements to the customers.
Question 5:
L&T demonstrates a strong commitment to cybersecurity and data privacy through comprehensive policies and strategic initiatives:
a) Data Privacy framework outlines the collection, use and protection of personal information. The policy emphasizes the secure storage of personal data on password- and firewall-protected servers. It also acknowledges the inherent risks of internet data transmission and advises users accordingly. Additionally, the policy addresses the use of cookies and provides guidance on managing them.
b) Cybersecurity Initiatives: A well-defined Cyber Crisis Management Plan is in place to respond to critical incidents. Incident response follows a structured life-cycle detection, containment, investigation, mitigation, recovery and reporting to ensure timely and effective action. A ‘Defence in depth’ strategy has been implemented, through Firewalls, Web Application Firewall (WAF), Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR), Data Loss Prevention (DLP) and Privileged Access Management (PAM). Vulnerability assessments are conducted regularly and a 24x7 Security Operations Centre (SOC) uses Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) tools for continuous monitoring, detection and responding to cyber threats.
